title: Kaiser Wilhelm Memorial Church - Quick Summary
items: **The Kaiser Wilhelm Memorial Church is Berlin’s “hollow tooth.”** A broken church tower from 1943, kept as a war memorial, with a striking modern blue-glass church built right next to it on Breitscheidplatz in City West.
**Entry is free, and there are two interiors.** The **New Church** (the blue octagon) and the **Memorial Hall** in the base of the old tower are both open to walk into at no charge.
**Hours are the one thing that trips people up.** New Church daily about 10:00 to 18:00; the old-tower Memorial Hall runs Mon to Sat 10:00 to 18:00 but only opens at **12:00 on Sundays**.
**Avoid Sunday morning.** Services at 10:00 and 18:00 pause casual visits, so a Sunday before noon can feel closed. A weekday late morning or afternoon is the easy window.
**Getting there is simple.** It is a three-minute walk from **S+U Zoologischer Garten** (U2/U9, S3/S5/S7/S9/S75), and just as close to U Kurfürstendamm (U1/U9), beside KaDeWe and the Ku’damm.

**Worth about 30 minutes.** Blue glass inside the new church, golden imperial mosaics and wartime photos in the old tower, then straight into City West shopping. After dark it glows blue and gold from the square.
